title: podcast-blueprint Layer 1 classification.json
description: Schema for the Layer 1 (scan/classify) output of the podcast-blueprint agent. Locked by operator design 2026-05-20. The eleven required fields encode every classification signal that downstream layers (Layer 2 episode-planner, Layer 3 convention-emitter) and the orchestrator (model-upgrade decision, series-config.yaml patch for P23 Episode Format Contract) consume.

schema_version.description: Bumped only on schema-breaking change. Validators refuse on mismatch.

book_slug.description: Kebab-case book slug; matches the directory name under content/podcast/library/books/.

source_signature.description: SHA-256 of the refined-english.md the classifier read. Caching/re-classification key.

classified_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp; set by the tooling, not by the model.

genre_primary.description: The dominant genre signal. Drives recommended_episode_planning_mode.

density_score.description: 0.0 = sparse narrative (e.g. memoir); 1.0 = dense argumentation with high cross-reference (e.g. exegesis). Drives recommended_model_for_layer_2 (0.0–0.33 Haiku, 0.34–0.66 Sonnet, 0.67–1.0 Opus by default; classifier may override based on cross_reference_load or vocabulary_contestedness).

structural_units.description: Ordered list of structural unit names as they appear in the source. Examples: ["babs", "fasls"] for Arabic polemics; ["chapters"] for memoirs; ["letters"] for epistolary.

cross_reference_load.description: How much the text depends on intratextual + intertextual references. High → favors Opus for Layer 2 (cross-episode anti-repetition needs richer planning).

vocabulary_contestedness.description: How much technical vocabulary is contested across traditions. High → favors Opus for Layer 2 (esoteric-concept analogical framing per P23 §8 is high-stakes).

recommended_model_for_layer_2.description: Layer 1's recommendation for the model used by Layers 2 + 3. Orchestrator honors unless operator passes --force-model. Default mapping: low density narrative → Haiku; standard non-fiction → Sonnet; high-density / high cross-reference / contested vocabulary → Opus.

recommended_audience_profile.description: Layer 1's recommendation for P23 audience_profile. Operator confirms via --approve-blueprint; on approval, this value is written into <book>/series-config.yaml, activating P23's Episode Format Contract.

recommended_source_tradition.description: Tradition-slug pointing at content/_shared/traditions/<slug>.md; null if the source does not map to a known tradition. Operator can override before --approve-blueprint.

recommended_episode_planning_mode.description: The episode-structure planning mode Layer 2 will use. tribunal_arc for polemic_tribunal; chronological for memoir; problem_solution for self_help; vignette_grid for essay_collection; dialectical_pairs for didactic_dialogue. Exegesis + epistle map to chronological by default but can use vignette_grid for non-linear sources.

rationale.description: Why this classification. MUST cite at least one specific signal from refined-english.md (e.g., 'opens with judicial framing on §3.1 — three named accusers structuring the polemic; cross-reference load high from chained Quranic citations'). NO generic boilerplate.
